axiom: The prompt you wrote is not the prompt the model received. Inspect the rendered final prompt before any other debug move.

operations.trigger: DSPy Predict/ChainOfThought/ReAct returned wrong output OR compile run hung mid-trial
operations.action: Call dspy.inspect_history(n=1) (or lm.inspect_history(n=3) for a specific LM instance)
operations.output: Last N LM invocations printed as system/user/assistant blocks plus raw response
operations.limitations: LM calls only; not retrievers/tools/subgraphs. Layer with mlflow.dspy.autolog for full pipeline.

operations.trigger: LangChain chain/agent produced unexpected output and need full prompt+response visibility
operations.action: from langchain.globals import set_debug, set_verbose; set_debug(True) (max) or set_verbose(True) (lighter)
operations.output: Console-printed prompt/response for every LM call in process
operations.limitations: Process-global scope; per-call alternative is StdOutCallbackHandler in callbacks=. Layer 2 view only; combine with OPENAI_LOG=debug for Layer 3.

operations.trigger: LangGraph thread produced wrong final answer; need per-step state including LM input at step k
operations.action: list(graph.get_state_history(config)) to read state per checkpoint; optionally graph.update_state + graph.invoke(None, config={'checkpoint_id': ...}) to fork
operations.output: StateSnapshot list with values (messages = LM input), config, next, metadata
operations.limitations: Requires a checkpointer configured. invoke(None, config=...) re-executes nodes — LM calls and tools fire again and pay cost. Reading history is free.

operations.trigger: CrewAI agent picked wrong tool / looped / wrong output; need per-step visibility
operations.action: Agent(..., verbose=True, step_callback=fn) and Crew(..., verbose=True). fn receives AgentAction or AgentFinish per step.
operations.output: Verbose: narrative thoughts + tool I/O + final answer. step_callback: structured AgentAction objects.
operations.limitations: CrewAI internal log is thin; production needs Langtrace/OpenLIT/AgentOps/MLflow autolog in addition.

operations.trigger: Aider applied wrong edit / mis-identified files / behaved unexpectedly
operations.action: /diff (last turn diff), /tokens (budget breakdown), /ls (file set), /map (repo-map). CLI: aider --verbose for full rendered prompt.
operations.output: Per-turn diff, token allocation, file membership, full prompt (verbose mode).
operations.limitations: Aider auto-commits — git diff HEAD~1 also works. Verbose is voluminous; pipe to file.

operations.trigger: Calling client.chat.completions.create directly; response is wrong; no framework templating layer
operations.action: export OPENAI_LOG=debug (or =info). Sets stdlib logging level inside the SDK.
operations.output: Stderr: HTTP method, URL, headers, request body, response status, response body.
operations.security_caveat: CRITICAL: at debug level, the Authorization header (API key) is logged in plaintext. Strip before sharing; never commit debug logs.

operations.trigger: Calling client.messages.create directly; response is wrong
operations.output: Same shape as op-006 — stderr request/response logging.

operations.trigger: Need full raw HTTP request body without OPENAI_LOG noise / API-key leak / formatting issues
operations.action: Pass custom httpx.Client with event_hooks={'request': [log_fn]} to the SDK via http_client= parameter
operations.output: Clean structured JSON dump of every request body; no key leakage unless you log headers.
operations.limitations: Response body in event hook needs resp.read() first.

operations.trigger: Token/latency spike after adding tools; model 'got dumber'; context-window error before real input
operations.action: Dump prompt (op-001 to op-007); count tokens in tool-definitions block alone
operations.output: Concrete token cost per tool; identification of compression candidates
operations.fix_options: Compress tool descriptions; drop verbose enum lists; lazy-load tools by routing

operations.trigger: Production bug; no local repro; need to inspect a real user thread
operations.action: Open the trace UI for the configured platform; navigate to the failing run; click into the LM call; view rendered prompt and response
operations.output: Production prompt/response ready for diff against expectation

operations.trigger: Found bad rendered prompt; need reproduction + regression test
operations.action: Save dumped prompt to tests/fixtures/prompt-bug-<id>.txt; add assertion that future renders do not contain the bad pattern
operations.output: Snapshot-style regression test that pins prompt rendering, not just model output

anti_patterns: Changing the prompt before inspecting it (#1 mistake)
Trusting source code matches what was sent
Inspecting at PromptTemplate.format level instead of SDK/HTTPX wire level
Leaving set_debug(True) or OPENAI_LOG=debug on in production
Replaying production threads via graph.invoke(None, config=...) — re-pays LM cost
Inspecting only the prompt, ignoring the response (truncation/refusal lives in response)
Using inspect_history as only observability for multi-component pipelines
Eyeballing long prompts instead of diffing against known-good
Reasoning about why the prompt is 'fine' without re-rendering after each change
Sharing a debug log without scrubbing API keys
boundaries: Pre-call errors (import/auth/network) — nothing to inspect, fix infra first
Prompt authoring from scratch — no rendered prompt exists yet
Eval-time aggregate metrics — wrong granularity, use eval/langsmith
Streaming-only debugging — framework streaming hooks better than post-hoc inspect
Already have trace UI configured — SOP applies, action becomes 'open the trace'

cross_framework_first_move.production_trace: Open run in LangSmith / LangFuse / Phoenix / MLflow
